title: Admin can grant verified badges on user profiles
description: The system shall allow an authenticated admin with user management privileges to grant a verified badge on a selected user profile.
rationale: To manage the verification status of users and ensure credibility within the system.
business_value: Improves user trust by clearly indicating verified users, which can enhance engagement and functionality.

source.description: As an admin I need to grant or revoke verified badges on user profiles So that I can manage the verification status of users to ensure credibility

acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated admin with user management privileges, When I select a user profile and choose to grant a verified badge, Then the user's profile displays a verified badge.

Given I am an authenticated admin with user management privileges, When I select a user profile that currently has a verified badge and choose to revoke it, Then the verified badge is removed from the user's profile.
preconditions: The system must be operational and accessible by authenticated admins with user management privileges.
The selected user profile must exist in the system.
postconditions: The user's profile must reflect the change in verification status (granted or revoked badge).
Any relevant logs or audit trails must record the action taken by the admin.
functional_constraints: The badge grant and revoke functionality must be idempotent to avoid unintended side effects.
The system must handle concurrent requests from multiple admins efficiently.
security_requirements: All actions related to granting or revoking badges must be logged for audit purposes.
Access to the user management interface must be restricted to authenticated admins with appropriate privileges.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The badge grant and revoke operations must complete within 200ms under normal conditions.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time, with no more than one hour downtime per month.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must support up to 1000 concurrent users managing badges without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages and feedback should be clear and concise, guiding admins through the process.

edge_cases: What happens when an admin attempts to grant a badge to a non-existent user profile?
How does the system handle concurrent requests for granting or revoking badges by multiple admins?
What if the admin tries to revoke a badge that is not currently assigned to the user?
dependencies: User Management Interface (UMI) must be operational and accessible.
Audit logging system must be in place to record all actions related to badge grants and revocations.

interfaces.name: Admin User Management Interface
interfaces.description: The interface through which admins can select a user profile and choose to grant or revoke verified badges.

interfaces.name: User Database
interfaces.description: The database where user profiles are stored, including the verified badge status.

interfaces.name: Audit Logging API
interfaces.description: API used to log actions related to granting and revoking badges for audit purposes.

test_cases.description: Granting a verified badge to an existing user profile.
test_cases.expected_result: The user's profile displays the verified badge, and logs are recorded indicating the action.

test_cases.description: Revoking a verified badge from a user profile that has one.
test_cases.expected_result: The verified badge is removed from the user's profile, and logs are recorded indicating the action.

test_cases.description: Attempting to grant a badge to a non-existent user profile.
test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed, indicating that the user profile does not exist.

test_cases.description: Concurrent requests for granting badges by multiple admins.
test_cases.expected_result: Badge grants complete successfully without conflict, and logs are recorded for each action.
